content: 2024–2025 state-of-the-art active learning agent memory can be restructured around a curiosity-driven retrieval gate that dynamically prioritizes which new unlabeled experiences to add to long-term memory, rather than statically pre-allocating memory slots or adding all encountered experiences by default. This design leverages retrieval-augmented architecture’s core strength of fast, context-aware lookup to enable active learning agents to proactively seek out and store only high-uncertainty, high-information experiences that reduce the agent’s prediction error, cutting memory bloat while improving out-of-distribution generalization.
